New resourceJune 5, 2026
New recurring resource
Compliance Corner Week 1:
Scope of Appointment
SOA requirements, the 48-hour rule, Permission to Contact expiration, and what's changing October 1.
What is a Scope of Appointment and when it's required
The 48-hour rule and exceptions for walk-ins and inbound calls
SOA validity (12 months) and storage requirements
Permission to Contact expiration windows by product line
View Compliance Corner
IMN Compliance Corner
Scope of Appointment
48-Hour Rule
48 hrs between SOA completion and scheduled appointment
Exceptions: walk-ins, last 4 days of enrollment period
SOA Validity and Storage
Valid 12 months. Keep all SOAs on file even if no sale.
PTC Expiration
MA and Part D leads: 12 months from signature
Must re-establish PTC after expiration
5 of 8View full Compliance Corner
Industry newsJune 5, 2026
Nick Tatge and Jesse Hendon
2027 Commissions and Co-op
Nick Tatge - Commission Schedule
"The 2027 commission schedule is out! $725/$363 is the national rate for most states."
$725
MA Initial
$363
MA Renewal
$130
PDP Initial
$65
PDP Renewal
730 reach - 26 reactions - 19 comments
Jesse Hendon - The bigger picture
"The real variable heading into AEP 2027 is carrier marketing investment, not commission rates. A 10-15% cut in co-op does not make headlines but absolutely shows up in what it costs to acquire a new client."
468 reach - 10 reactions
Member reactions
"I feel like this is nice optics by CMS but we will likely still have non-comm or reduced-comm plans."
- Chris Fong
"We need all agents to have the same commission levels. Inflation is all across America and the cost of living is affecting all agents."
- Duaine Owings
"Are there even any PDPs that pay commission anymore?"
- Louisa James
Jesse's takeaway
Build organic acquisition channels now. Do not depend on carrier co-op reimbursements heading into AEP.
